# Vercel integration (correct + secure)

Integrate Vercel into this Next.js/TypeScript + LangGraph project so it is **correct and secure by construction**: every inbound webhook is signature-verified, every secret lives in the right place, every API call is scoped and idempotent, and test vs production paths are explicit. This skill is concept-first and points at the live docs — Vercel changes specifics often, so **pull current docs before writing code**.

## Pull current docs first

Fetch these (they carry a `last_updated` date — check it):

- Webhooks setup & events: https://vercel.com/docs/webhooks
- Webhooks API (event types, securing-webhooks): https://vercel.com/docs/webhooks/webhooks-api
- **Signature verification (the authoritative `x-vercel-signature` snippet)**: https://vercel.com/docs/headers/request-headers#x-vercel-signature
- Build a Marketplace integration (client ID/secret, scopes, redirect/config URLs): https://vercel.com/docs/integrations/create-integration
- Sensitive (non-readable) environment variables: https://vercel.com/docs/environment-variables/sensitive-environment-variables
- REST API auth (Bearer tokens, team scope, rate limits): https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api
- Protection Bypass for Automation (Playwright against protected deploys): https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection/methods-to-bypass-deployment-protection/protection-bypass-automation

## Core concepts (timeless)

**Where this project touches Vercel.** The Vercel/v0 Next.js app hosts the review dashboard and thin API routes; those routes are the inbound surface (webhook receivers, resume-approval endpoints). The heavy LangGraph/Playwright/ffmpeg work runs on the GitHub Actions runner, not on Vercel functions — keep long jobs off serverless. Vercel deployment webhooks can *trigger* a release run; treat them as untrusted until verified.

**Auth & secrets — three distinct credentials, do not mix them up.**
1. *Webhook signing secret* — used only to verify inbound `x-vercel-signature`. Account webhooks: the secret shown once at webhook creation. Integration webhooks: your Integration/Client Secret from the Integration Console.
2. *REST API access token* — `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, scoped to a team; pass `teamId`/`slug` as a query param for team-owned resources. Create with least privilege, set an expiration, rotate on a schedule.
3. *Automation bypass secret* — `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET`, lets Playwright/CI reach protected deployments.

Store all three as **sensitive environment variables** (non-readable once saved; production/preview only — *not* the Development environment). Never expose secrets via `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` (those are inlined into the browser bundle). Read secrets only in route handlers / server actions / server code. Prefer rotating via the Integration Console **Rotate Secret** button over editing in place.

**Webhook signature verification (the one mechanic you must get exactly right).** Vercel signs the **raw request body** with **HMAC-SHA1** (hex digest) and sends it as `x-vercel-signature`. To verify, recompute `HMAC-SHA1(rawBody, secret)` and compare with `crypto.timingSafeEqual` (constant-time). Critical: you must hash the **unparsed** body —
- App Router: `const rawBody = await request.text()` then `JSON.parse` *after* verifying.
- Pages Router / `@vercel/node`: set `export const config = { api: { bodyParser: false } }` and read with `raw-body`; the framework body parser will otherwise mutate bytes and break the HMAC.

Compact reference (mirror the current doc snippet exactly):
```ts
import crypto from 'crypto';
export async function POST(request: Request) {
  const secret = process.env.VERCEL_WEBHOOK_SECRET!;
  const sig = request.headers.get('x-vercel-signature');
  const raw = await request.text();
  const expected = crypto.createHmac('sha1', secret).update(raw).digest('hex');
  if (!sig || sig.length !== expected.length ||
      !crypto.timingSafeEqual(Buffer.from(sig), Buffer.from(expected)))
    return Response.json({ error: 'Invalid signature' }, { status: 403 });
  const event = JSON.parse(raw); // { id, type, createdAt, payload, region }
}
```

**Idempotency & retry semantics.** Each delivery carries a unique `id` and a `type` (e.g. `deployment.succeeded`, `deployment.error`, `marketplace.invoice.paid`). Vercel does not guarantee exactly-once — design the receiver to be **idempotent**: dedupe on `id` (persist seen IDs in Aurora, e.g. a unique constraint, before acting). Return 2xx **fast** (after verify + enqueue) so Vercel doesn't treat slow responses as failures; never run LangGraph/Bedrock work inline in the webhook handler — record the event and resume the graph `thread_id` out of band.

**Error semantics.** Return 403 on bad/missing signature, 400 on malformed payload, 2xx once accepted (even if downstream is async). For outbound REST calls, honor `X-RateLimit-Remaining`/`X-RateLimit-Reset` and back off on `429`; retry idempotent reads, be careful retrying writes.

**Test/sandbox vs production.** Sensitive env vars exist only in production/preview — local/dev needs a separate non-sensitive secret (e.g. a dev webhook + `.env.local`, never committed). Use **preview deployments** plus Protection Bypass for Automation to let Playwright run against real protected deploys; `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` is injected as a system env var. Header method is preferred (`x-vercel-protection-bypass`); the query-param method exists for services that can't send custom headers — prefer the header to keep the secret out of URLs/logs. Regenerating/deleting the bypass secret invalidates existing deployments until you redeploy.

## Checklist

- [ ] Pulled the current docs above; confirmed `x-vercel-signature` is still HMAC-SHA1 over the raw body.
- [ ] Webhook handler reads the **raw** body (App Router `request.text()`, or `bodyParser:false` + `raw-body`) and verifies **before** `JSON.parse`.
- [ ] Signature compared with `timingSafeEqual` (length-checked first); returns 403 on mismatch.
- [ ] Correct signing secret chosen: account-webhook secret vs Integration/Client Secret vs drain secret.
- [ ] Receiver is idempotent — dedupes on event `id` (DB unique constraint) and returns 2xx quickly; no LangGraph/Bedrock work inline.
- [ ] All three credential types stored as **sensitive** env vars (prod/preview), none in `NEXT_PUBLIC_*`, none committed; dev uses separate non-sensitive secrets.
- [ ] REST tokens are team-scoped, least-privilege, expiring; calls pass `teamId`; `429`/rate-limit headers handled with backoff.
- [ ] Playwright reaches protected deploys via `VERCEL_AUTOMATION_BYPASS_SECRET` (header method); config throws if the secret is absent.
- [ ] Integration secrets rotated via Console **Rotate Secret**; rotation runbook noted (redeploy needed for bypass-secret changes).
- [ ] Verified end-to-end against a **preview** deployment, not just locally.

## Gotchas

- **Body parsing silently breaks the HMAC.** Any framework/middleware that parses or re-serializes the body before you hash it changes the bytes — signature will never match. Hash `request.text()` / `raw-body` output, parse afterward. This is the #1 webhook failure.
- **Wrong secret for the source.** Account webhooks, integration webhooks, and log drains use *different* secrets. An integration webhook verified against the account-webhook secret fails 100% of the time — use the Integration/Client Secret from the Console.
- **`NEXT_PUBLIC_` leaks secrets into the browser bundle.** v0/Next inlines these at build time; a token placed there ships to every visitor. Server-only secrets must have no `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix.
- **Sensitive env vars can't be created in Development and can't be read back.** You can't fetch the value later (only overwrite), and you can't mark Development sensitive — plan dev secrets separately. Build logs redact sensitive values only when ≥32 chars (shorter secrets may print in logs).
- **Webhooks are not exactly-once and ordering isn't guaranteed.** Without `id`-based dedupe you'll double-process releases (duplicate content artifacts, duplicate Aurora rows). Persist seen IDs.
- **Slow webhook handlers look like failures.** Doing Bedrock/LangGraph/Playwright work inline blows the response window; accept + enqueue + resume the graph `thread_id` asynchronously.
- **Rotating the automation bypass secret invalidates live deployments.** The value is baked at build time, so old preview/prod deployments keep the old secret until you redeploy — rotate then redeploy, or Playwright 401s.
- **Bypass secret in a query string ends up in logs/referrers.** The query-param method is a fallback for header-less services; prefer the `x-vercel-protection-bypass` header and store the value in an env var, never hardcoded in the webhook URL.
- **Long jobs don't belong on Vercel functions.** Serverless/Edge time and size limits will kill ffmpeg/Playwright/LangGraph runs — keep those on the GitHub Actions runner; Vercel hosts only the dashboard and thin verify-and-enqueue routes.
- **Don't trust geo/IP headers for auth.** `x-forwarded-for` is overwritten by Vercel (anti-spoofing) and `x-vercel-ip-*` are best-effort — signature verification, not IP allowlisting, is the security boundary.
- _Add project-specific failures here as you hit them._
